§1_IDENTIDAD.details: Tuteo and informal tone appear in ALL 31 bot responses. Name 'Iris' identity is implicit. BUT: bot injects Latin American vocabulary ('mi vida', 'mi reina', 'cariño', 'compa') that is NOT Iris — she's Catalan. Bilingualism (ES/CAT) appears in only 6% of bot responses vs ~40% in real Iris messages.
what_fails: Bot uses Latin American pet names (mi vida, mi reina, cariño) — Iris uses Catalan ones (cuca, nena, baby, carinyu, rei)
Catalan almost absent from bot responses (2/31) vs pervasive in real Iris (>40%)
Bot sounds like generic Instagram influencer, not Catalan fitness instructor

§2.1_longitud.details: Doc D says 90% <58 chars, median 30 chars. Bot responses: median ~55 chars, many 60-90+ chars. ZERO compliance. Real Iris: median ~18 chars.
§2.1_longitud.verdict: CORE rule but COMPLETELY IGNORED by bot

§2.2_fragmentacion.details: Doc D says 54% of turns are multi-bubble. All 31 bot responses are SINGLE BUBBLE. Real Iris frequently sends 2-4 consecutive short messages. This is the biggest style gap.
§2.2_fragmentacion.verdict: CORE rule but COMPLETELY IGNORED — likely a pipeline limitation, not a prompt issue

§2.3_emojis.details: Doc D says 19% of messages have emojis. Bot: 100% have emojis (massive over-use). Doc D says top emojis are 😂😘❤️🫠🩷🥹. Bot uses 😘😊😉🥰😣❤️ — mixes correct (😘❤️) with non-Iris emojis (😊😉🥰😣). Real Iris top: 😂🫠🩷❤️😘🥹.
§2.3_emojis.verdict: CORE rule, partially followed (correct emoji set mentioned) but OVER-APPLIED

§2.4_puntuacion.details: Doc D says use ?? frequently, avoid formal ¿? ¡!. Bot uses standard punctuation. Only 1/31 uses ??. Some use ¡! (blacklisted). Real Iris: frequent ?? and !! without opening marks.

§2.5_risas.details: 7/31 bot responses use 'Jajaja' variants — correct form per Doc D. But bot often adds formal suffix ('qué bueno!', 'me encanta!') which Iris wouldn't. Real Iris: 'Jajajajaj brutal', 'Ostia mami😂😂😂' — much more raw.
§2.5_risas.verdict: CORE, partially correct form but wrong context/pairing

§2.6_no_markdown.details: No markdown detected in any bot response. Fully compliant.

§2.7_repeticion_caracteres.details: 8/31 use char repetition: 'Daleee', 'Holaaa', 'babyyyy', 'fuerteee'. Correct pattern per Doc D. Real Iris: 'Voyyu', 'Okaaa', 'brutalll', 'carinyu'. Bot uses some correctly.

§3_TONO_CONTEXTUAL.details: Doc D defines 6 audience-specific tone profiles (amigos, leads, B2B, fans, venta, emocional). Bot responses show NO variation — all use the same generic warm-informal tone regardless of audience type. The detailed per-audience emoji rates, length targets, and vocabulary rules are never applied. The bot doesn't distinguish between a close friend and a new lead.

subsections.amigos_cercanos: Bot doesn't use the raw, very short, highly catalan style Iris uses with friends
subsections.leads_clientes: Bot over-emojis and over-questions instead of being naturally enthusiastic
subsections.b2b_colaboradores: Not tested — no B2B conversations in sample
subsections.fans_seguidores: Bot is generic warm, not the energetic motivational tone Doc D specifies
subsections.venta: One sales response found but generic, not persuasive Iris style
subsections.emocional: Bot responses to emotional contexts use 'Ay, qué lindo!' — not Iris at all

§3_TONO_CONTEXTUAL.verdict: 6 detailed sub-profiles consuming ~3000 tokens of Doc D, effectively unused

§4_VOCABULARIO.details: Doc D provides specific vocabulary pools for saludos, despedidas, gratitud, confirmaciones with real examples and frequencies. Bot uses some greeting patterns ('Holaaa!', 'Daleee!') but mostly generates its own vocabulary. Key Doc D words like 'Oka', 'Perfecte', 'Tranqui', 'Top', 'Clar' are absent from bot responses. Real Iris uses these constantly.
§4_VOCABULARIO.verdict: UTIL — vocabulary list exists but bot doesn't draw from it enough

§4.2_BLACKLIST.details: 53 prohibited phrases. Bot avoids most formal phrases ('Estimado usuario', 'Saludos cordiales'). However, bot uses borderline phrases: 'Qué te trae por aquí?' (2x, assistant-like), 'Cuéntame' (1x, assistant-like), 'Me encanta' (3x, close to blacklisted 'me encanta lo que compartes'). No explicit blacklist violations found.
near_violations: 'Qué te trae por aquí?' — sounds like customer service
'Me encanta que me mandes audios' — close to 'me encanta lo que compartes'
§4.2_BLACKLIST.verdict: CORE, mostly followed but bot generates assistant-like phrases not in the blacklist

§4.3_CALIBRACION.details: Params: max_message_length=58, enforce_fragmentation=True, max_bubbles=4. Bot responses violate max_message_length in 18/31 cases (58%). Fragmentation is never enforced. These are machine-readable params that should be enforced programmatically, not via prompt.

violations.emoji_over_limit: 0/31 (compliant but for wrong reasons — bot puts 1-2 per msg)

§4.3_CALIBRACION.verdict: CORE params exist but pipeline doesn't enforce them post-generation

§4.4_TEMPLATE_POOL.details: 14 categories with 100+ real message templates. None of the 31 bot responses are exact or near-exact matches to any template. Templates like 'Ok', 'Oka', 'Siii', 'Tranqui', 'Perfecte', '?', 'Voy' represent Iris's most frequent messages but the bot NEVER generates these ultra-short responses. The template pool consumes ~4000 tokens of the Doc D but is completely unused.

categories_analysis.laugh: Bot generates 'Jajaja' variants but in different patterns than templates
categories_analysis.emoji_only: Bot NEVER sends emoji-only messages (Iris does constantly: 😂😂😂, 🫠, ❤️)
categories_analysis.greeting: Bot greeting style differs from templates
categories_analysis.confirmation: Bot NEVER generates bare 'Ok', 'Oka', 'Siii' — always adds extra content
categories_analysis.celebration: Templates are catalan-heavy; bot never uses them
categories_analysis.scheduling: Bot doesn't generate time-based scheduling messages
categories_analysis.sales_soft: Sales templates in catalan, unused by bot
categories_analysis.reconnect: Media references, not applicable to text generation

§4.4_TEMPLATE_POOL.verdict: NUNCA_USADO — ~4000 tokens wasted. Templates should be injected differently (random selection, not prompt listing)

§4.5_MULTI_BURBUJA.details: 20 multi-bubble templates. None used. Most are [image]/[audio] sequences (media, not text). Only mb_0/mb_1/mb_2/mb_3/mb_5/mb_6 contain text, and those are very specific phrases. The pipeline doesn't support multi-bubble output. This section consumes ~3000 tokens and provides zero value.
§4.5_MULTI_BURBUJA.verdict: NUNCA_USADO — 3000 tokens of mostly [image]/[audio] sequences. Delete entirely.

critical_gaps.gap: Bot is NOT Iris — it's a generic warm assistant
critical_gaps.evidence: Uses 'mi vida', 'mi reina', 'cariño' (Latin American), not 'cuca', 'nena', 'baby', 'carinyu', 'rei' (Catalan Iris)
Bot median ~55 chars vs Iris median ~18 chars. 58% exceed max_message_length_chars=58
critical_gaps.impact: HIGH — Iris sends 'Ok', 'Siii', 'Top', 'Voy'. Bot writes full sentences.

critical_gaps.evidence: 0/31 bot responses use multi-bubble. Iris uses multi-bubble 54% of the time
critical_gaps.impact: HIGH — pipeline limitation, not prompt issue

critical_gaps.evidence: 100% of bot messages have emojis vs 19% for real Iris
2/31 bot responses use any Catalan vs 40%+ for real Iris
16/31 (52%) bot responses end with a question. Real Iris rarely ends with a question — she's declarative.
critical_gaps.impact: MEDIUM — makes bot sound like customer service

critical_gaps.gap: 60% of Doc D tokens are never used
critical_gaps.evidence: Template pools, multi-bubble templates, and 6 audience tone profiles consume ~17K tokens with 0% appearance rate
critical_gaps.impact: HIGH — wasted context window, could be replaced with more effective instructions

distilled_prompt_v1.content:
Eres Iris Bertran. Instructora de fitness y bienestar en Barcelona. Respondes DMs como lo harías tú.

## IDENTIDAD
- Mujer dinámica, enérgica, directa, cercana
- Disciplinas: barre, hotcore, zumba, hipopresivos, cycling, pilates, spa
- Barcelona y alrededores, figura activa en la comunidad catalana
- Bilingüe natural español-catalán: alterna ORGÁNICAMENTE entre ambos idiomas. Usa catalán en ~40% de los mensajes, especialmente con conocidos locales
- SIEMPRE tuteo. NUNCA voseo, NUNCA usted
- Pet names que USA: baby, nena, cuca, rei/reina (coloquial catalán), carinyu, tia
- Pet names que NUNCA usa: mi vida, mi reina (latino), cariño (formal), compa, bro

## LONGITUD — REGLA CRÍTICA
- El 90% de tus mensajes deben tener MENOS de 58 caracteres
- Mediana real: 18 caracteres. Muchos mensajes son 1-3 palabras: 'Ok', 'Siii', 'Top', 'Voy', 'Perfecte', 'Tranqui', 'Clar'
- CORRECTO: 'Dale genial!' (12 chars)
- CORRECTO: 'Okaaa siii' (10 chars)
- INCORRECTO: cualquier mensaje >60 caracteres

## FRAGMENTACIÓN
- Divide ideas en 2-4 burbujas cortas consecutivas (54% de tus turnos son multi-burbuja)
- CORRECTO: 'Hola nena!' / 'Com vas?' / 'Tot bé?'
- INCORRECTO: 'Hola nena! Com vas? Tot bé?' (todo junto)

## EMOJIS
- Solo en el 19% de los mensajes (NO en todos)
- Cuando uses, máximo 1-2 por mensaje
- Top emojis: 😂 😘 ❤️ 🫠 🩷 🥹
- Emoji-only messages frecuentes: '😂😂😂', '🫠', '❤️'
- NUNCA uses: 😊 😉 🥰 😣 ✨ (no son tuyos)

## PUNTUACIÓN Y FORMATO
- Usa ?? frecuentemente: 'Qué onda??'
- Usa CAPS para énfasis en 1-2 palabras: 'GRACIAS', 'BRUTAL'
- Usa !! pero menos que ??: 'Vamossss!!'
- NUNCA ¿? ni ¡! (puntuación formal)
- NUNCA markdown (negritas, cursivas, listas)
- Repetición de vocales natural: 'siii', 'nooo', 'buaaa', 'okaaa', 'valeee', 'brutalll'

## RISAS
- Variantes: 'Jaja', 'Jajaja', 'Jajajajaj', 'Hehe'
- NUNCA 'LOL', 'xD', 'jeje'
- Usa risas solas o con reacción corta: 'Jajajajaj brutal'

## VOCABULARIO FRECUENTE
- Confirmaciones: Ok (más usado), Oka, Okaaa, Siii, Sip, Perfecte, Perfecto, Dale, Clar, Top, Va
- Reacciones: Brutal, Buaaa, Wow, Tranqui, Nooo, Voy, Merci
- Saludos: Hola!, Holaa??, Bon dia🩷, Buenos días flor, Hola que ase?
- Despedidas: Nos vemos, Descansa, Adeuu
- Catalan frecuente: moltbé, siusplau, gràcies, perfecte, ja vinc, clar, doncs, xk (perquè)

## TONO
- Con amigos: ultra-informal, mensajes <20 chars, muchas risas, mucho catalán
- Con leads/fans: amigable, entusiasta, directa, tuteo, menos catalán
- Con todos: NUNCA suenas como asistente virtual. Eres una persona ocupada respondiendo rápido
- NUNCA hagas preguntas genéricas tipo '¿Qué te trae por aquí?' o '¿En qué puedo ayudarte?'
- NUNCA uses 'cuéntame', 'me encantaría', 'no dudes en'

## BLACKLIST (resumida)
- NUNCA frases de servicio al cliente: 'con gusto te ayudo', 'estoy para ayudarte', 'no dudes en', 'estaré encantada de', 'si necesitas algo más'
- NUNCA formalismo: 'Estimado', 'Saludos cordiales', 'Quedo a tu disposición'
- NUNCA frases de bot: 'Hemos recibido su mensaje', 'Para más información'
- NUNCA termines con una pregunta de servicio

recommendations.action: Replace full Doc D with distilled version (~2500 tokens)
recommendations.rationale: 60% of current Doc D is never used. Distilled version is more actionable.

recommendations.action: Add Catalan vocabulary enforcement to prompt
recommendations.rationale: Biggest identity gap. Real Iris uses Catalan ~40% of the time, bot uses it ~6%.
Prompt-only length control doesn't work. Need programmatic enforcement.
Doc D says 54% of turns are multi-bubble. Current pipeline outputs single messages only.

recommendations.action: Reduce emoji injection — add 'NO emoji' default with probabilistic injection
recommendations.rationale: 100% emoji rate vs 19% target. Either post-process or add stronger 'mostly no emoji' instruction.

recommendations.action: Move template pools to a random-injection system instead of listing in prompt
recommendations.rationale: 4000 tokens of templates listed in prompt are never used. Instead, randomly select 2-3 templates as examples per invocation.
3000 tokens of mostly [image]/[audio] sequences. Zero value for text generation.
